strategy.name: Momentum Day Trading — Ross Cameron & Andrew Aziz Framework
sources: Ross Cameron (Warrior Trading) — Gap & Go, VWAP momentum strategy
Andrew Aziz (Bear Bull Traders) — Moving Average Trend strategy

indicators.ema_9: Primary short-term trend. Price above = bullish momentum. Enter pullbacks to this level.
indicators.ema_20: Stronger support/resistance than 9 EMA. Preferred stop-loss reference level.
indicators.ema_50: Medium-term trend. Acts as support in healthy uptrends.
indicators.ema_200: Major trend bias filter. Trade LONG only above 200 EMA. Trade SHORT only below 200 EMA.
indicators.vwap: Equilibrium line. Price above VWAP = buyers in control. Key entry/exit zone for intraday.
indicators.bollinger_bands_20_2: Volatility envelope. Buy near lower band, target middle or upper band.
indicators.macd_12_26_9: Momentum confirmation. Enter long when MACD line crosses above signal.
indicators.rsi_14: Overbought/oversold filter. Momentum trades: RSI 50–80 zone. Avoid above 80 or below 20.
indicators.volume: Confirms all breakouts. No volume = no trade.

bullish: Price is above the 200 EMA (Ross Cameron rule — only trade long above 200 EMA)
Price is above VWAP on the intraday chart
MACD line is above the signal line
bearish: Price is below VWAP on the intraday chart
MACD line is below the signal line
neutral: 9 EMA and 20 EMA are tangled or flat
RSI is between 45 and 55 with no clear direction

long: Price is above the 200 EMA (required bias filter)
Price pulled back to the 9 EMA or VWAP and is holding
MACD is above the signal line (momentum confirmed)
RSI is between 50 and 70 (room to run, not overbought)
Volume is above average on the entry candle
Enter on the next candle after the 9 EMA or VWAP holds as support
short: Price is below the 200 EMA (required bias filter)
Price rallied back to the 9 EMA or VWAP and is being rejected
MACD is below the signal line
Volume is above average on the rejection candle
Enter on the next candle after the 9 EMA or VWAP acts as resistance

exit_rules: Stop loss: just below the 20 EMA (longs) or just above the 20 EMA (shorts)
Move stop to break-even once trade is 1R in profit
Take partial profit (1/3 to 1/2 position) at first resistance or when RSI hits 70+
Trail remainder with the 9 EMA or 20 EMA
Hard exit: if price breaks and closes below the 20 EMA against your position
risk_rules: Minimum R:R of 2:1 before entering any trade
Maximum risk per trade: 1% of portfolio
No more than 2 open positions at once
If 2 consecutive losses occur, stop trading for the day
No trading in the first 5 minutes of market open (avoid the chop)
Only trade with above-average volume — no volume, no trade
notes: Strategy based on publicly documented frameworks from Ross Cameron (Warrior Trading) and Andrew Aziz (Bear Bull Traders). Core principle: trade with the trend (200 EMA bias), enter on pullbacks to VWAP or 9 EMA, confirm with volume and MACD, manage risk with the 20 EMA as stop reference.
